sections.title: 第一部分：單題（第1-21題，共21題）

items.question: Look at the picture.  There is ___ under the door.
items.explanation: 圖片顯示門縫下有信封被塞入，故選 (A) an envelope。

items.question: My ___ hurts so much that I cannot even turn my head.
items.explanation: 無法轉動頭部，表示脖子（neck）受傷，故選 (C)。

items.question: Our school basketball team won the national game last night.  We are so ___ them.
items.explanation: 球隊獲勝，我們感到很驕傲（proud of），故選 (B)。

items.question: Tomorrow is Sam's last day in the office.  Nobody knows why he decided to ___.
items.explanation: Sam 明天是最後一天在辦公室，表示他決定離開（leave）工作，故選 (B)。

items.question: It's not a good idea to go mountain climbing in this bad ___.  We should wait until the typhoon goes away.
items.explanation: 颱風來臨時天氣惡劣（bad weather），不適合爬山，故選 (D)。

items.question: Chris loves walking with Anna on snowy days, but Anna hates ___ very much.
items.explanation: hate 後面用 it 代替前文提到的 walking with Chris on snowy days，故選 (D)。

items.question: Lora likes to eat bananas that are already a little brown on the outside, and so ___ I.
items.explanation: likes 是一般動詞，so do I 表示「我也是（喜歡）」，故選 (B)。

items.question: Your refrigerator shouldn't be making loud noises now, but if it ___ does, just give me a call and I'll come check it again.
items.explanation: 「如果它仍然（still）有噪音的話」，表示可能持續出現的狀況，故選 (D)。

items.question: After winning money in the card game, Jay decided to try again.  He felt that he might also be ___ a second time.
items.explanation: 贏了一次後再試，希望再次幸運（lucky），故選 (C)。

items.question: The knife doesn't cut very well.  It's not as ___ as before.
items.explanation: 刀子切不好，表示不如以前鋒利（sharp），故選 (D)。

items.question: John will stay with his sister until he ___ an apartment.
items.explanation: until 引導的時間副詞子句，用現在式表示未來，故選 (C) finds。

items.question: Students ___ to go on the school trip should ask their parents first.
optionsZh: 想要的（關係代名詞子句）
items.explanation: 修飾 students 的關係子句，主格關係代名詞用 who，故選 (A) who want。

items.question: The temple sits alone in the mountains at a height of 3,000m ___ sea level.
items.explanation: above sea level 表示海拔高度，固定搭配，故選 (A)。

items.question: Patty spent several days planning to invite Charlie to dinner, ___ she couldn't say a word when they met.
items.explanation: 前後形成對比（計畫許久卻一句話也說不出口），用轉折連接詞 but，故選 (A)。

items.question: I can't tell you what I think of the movie because I ___ it.  I'll probably watch it this Saturday.
items.explanation: 尚未看過用現在完成式（haven't seen），且週六才要去看，故選 (C)。

items.question: The new guy at the help desk answers calls like a ___.  There are no ups and downs in his voice and you can't tell if he is happy or sad.
items.explanation: 聲音毫無起伏、分辨不出情緒，像機器人（robot），故選 (D)。

items.question: Jasmine planned to spend her summer in the country, but right after she got there, she started to ___ the noise in the city.
items.explanation: 到了鄉下反而開始想念（miss）城市的喧囂，出乎意料的對比，故選 (C)。

items.question: "Bad traffic" is perhaps the ___ excuse for being late when your boss knows it only takes you five minutes to walk to work.
items.explanation: 上班步行只需五分鐘卻說塞車，這是最糟糕的（worst）藉口，故選 (D)。

items.question: The housework in Mr. and Mrs. Wang's family ___ between them and their kids.  Everyone's got their own job to do.
items.explanation: housework 是不可數名詞，動詞用單數；家事被分配故用被動 is shared，故選 (A)。

items.question: I want to find another dentist because ___ pulled out a good tooth last time I went to him.
optionsZh: 我的牙醫（所有格代名詞）
items.explanation: mine 此處作主詞，代替 my dentist（我的牙醫），意思是「我的牙醫上次拔掉了一顆好牙」，故選 (C)。

items.question:
Mom: Linda, you've been playing computer games all evening!  Have you finished your report?
Linda: Well, ___ most of it this afternoon, and I'll finish it by Friday.
items.explanation: this afternoon 表示今天下午已完成的動作，用過去式 I did，故選 (B)。

sections.title: 第二部分：題組（第22-43題，共22題）

passages.passage:
It was around eight o'clock on Saturday night. Philip was at home with his little brother, Jason. Jason was hungry and kept crying. But Philip couldn't cook and their father was not home yet because he was working late in the office. So Philip decided to take Jason out for some food.

Half an hour later, when they came back home, Philip was surprised to find that their father was talking to the police and a woman outside their house. He didn't know who the woman was. She looked scared and stood behind the police. Philip's father was very angry and kept shouting, "I didn't do anything wrong! I forgot my keys and was just trying to get into MY OWN HOUSE!" But the police didn't believe him until Philip ran to them and explained everything.

The police told Philip that the woman called 110 when she saw Philip's father trying to get into a house through a window. That was how it all happened.

questions.question: Why did Philip go out?
options: To ask the police for help.
To buy food for his brother.
questions.explanation: Jason 餓了一直哭，Philip 沒辦法煮飯，所以決定帶弟弟出去買食物，故選 (D)。

questions.question: Why was Philip's father angry?
options: The woman was hiding from him.
The police didn't believe what he said.
Philip and his brother went out at night.
optionsZh: Philip 和弟弟晚上出去了
questions.explanation: 父親一直喊自己沒做錯，但警察不相信他是屋主，直到 Philip 回來解釋才解決，父親憤怒的原因是警察不信任他，故選 (C)。

passages.caption: Baker's Kitchen 每日新鮮麵包出爐時刻表

questions.question: Kevin is going to buy some fresh bread at Baker's Kitchen.  He loves white bread, his mom likes farm bread, his father enjoys bagels, and his sister eats only challah.  Which is the earliest possible time for him to get all these breads for his family?
questions.explanation: 白麵包10:30出爐、貝果11:30出爐、農場麵包和哈拉麵包均為15:30出爐。四種都備齊的最早時間為15:30後，選項中最接近的是（B）下午4點，故選 (B)。

questions.question: What do we know about Baker's Kitchen?
options: It is open five days a week.
Its breads are half price one hour before closing.
Its croissants and pretzels are sold on weekends.
Its members can save $100 when they shop on Fridays.
optionsZh: 關門前一小時麵包半價
可頌和椒鹽卷餅週末販售
questions.explanation: 週六日20:00關門，19:00後所有麵包半價（關門前一小時）；本店週一至日全年開放（非五天）；椒鹽卷餅僅週五販售；會員費100元享九折優惠，非省100元，故選 (B)。

passages.caption: 2021年綠色節慶地圖與交通資訊

questions.question: What is recommended to people who want to visit the festival?
options: Using the free festival bus service.
Visiting the festival on the weekend.
Entering Satyr's Park from Fox Street.
Parking in Garden Square and walking to the festival.
optionsZh: 搭乘免費節慶巴士服務
在Garden廣場停車後步行前往
questions.explanation: 資訊明確歡迎民眾在Goat Street站搭乘免費節慶巴士；Fox Street封閉無法進入；Garden Square為農夫市集不開放停車，故選 (A)。

questions.question: What can we learn about the farmers' market from the map?
options: The farmers' market is next to the flower market.
The farmers' market and the festival are on the same block.
You can go to the farmers' market by taking Bus No. 157 to Puppy Street.
The nearest metro station to the farmers' market is the Koala Street Station.
optionsZh: 農夫市集緊鄰花卉市場
農夫市集和節慶在同一個街區
搭157號公車到Puppy Street可到農夫市集
距農夫市集最近的地鐵站是Koala Street站
questions.explanation: 地圖顯示Garden Square（農夫市集）距Koala Street地鐵站最近；Bus No. 157未出現在官方交通資訊中，故選 (D)。

passages.passage:
Yan lived a good life in a big house. One day he invited a friend to dinner at his house. However, on the dinner table there was only one small dish of one small fish. The friend looked quietly at the fish. It was no bigger than a finger.
Then he asked Yan if he could borrow a lamp.
"What for?" Yan asked.
"Well, it's so dark in here," the friend said with a dry smile, "I can't see the other delicious dishes you've prepared for me."

Chang kept a lot of ducks and chickens on his farm. One day, his best friend came to visit him. At noon, Chang told his friend that he couldn't let him stay for lunch because there wasn't much food to eat. The friend looked out at Chang's farm animals for a moment. Then he asked Chang if he had a big knife.
"Yes, but what for?" Chang asked.
"I'm thinking about killing the horse I rode here so we'll have something for lunch," the friend said.
"But how are you going to go home without it?"
"Well, you wouldn't mind lending me one of your many ducks or chickens so I can ride it home, would you?"

questions.question: What kind of people do Yan's and Chang's friends most likely think Yan and Chang are?
questions.explanation: 顏家身家富裕卻只上一道小魚；張家養了大量家禽卻說沒食物招待。兩位朋友都用諷刺方式點出對方不愛分享，故選 (B)。

questions.question: What is "it" in the second story?
options: One of Chang's ducks or chickens.
questions.explanation: 朋友說要殺掉他騎來的馬作午餐，張問「你沒有它要怎麼回去？」中的 it 就是指那匹馬，故選 (B)。

passages.passage:
Usually, people visit bookstores to buy books. But those who walk into Cotoha come to see its owner, Chen Bing-Hong. They bring him books with different problems. Some have lost their covers, some are missing a few pages, and some have pages that are falling out. Chen can always fix the books and make them whole again.

Chen sees himself as a doctor. He says that books, like people, get sick and need help. In Taiwan, there are not many "doctors" like him, and most of them work for big libraries and museums. If big libraries and museums are hospitals, Chen's bookstore is a health center. Big libraries and museums fix important old books with serious problems, and Chen helps people with books that have smaller problems. However, the smaller size of the problems doesn't mean Chen's job is less important.

Chen gave an example of a science book he once worked on. It was very old and half of its cover was lost. Its owner still wanted to keep it because it was a gift from a teacher who helped him follow his dream of studying science. When he got his book back, he was very surprised at the new-old book. He felt he was brought back to the days with his teacher. Seeing the owner's smile made Chen happy, because smiles like that are what his magic is for.

questions.question: Why does the writer talk about doctors and a health center in the reading?
options: To talk about Chen's future plans.
To explain Chen's love for books.
To show why bookstores are important.
optionsZh: 解釋陳秉宏的服務內容
questions.explanation: 文章用醫生和健康中心比喻陳秉宏修書的服務——他像醫生一樣幫「生病的書」治病，故選 (A)。

questions.question: Why is Chen Bing-Hong's job important in the example he gave?
options: It allows people to get books as gifts.
It saves people money on new books.
It gives people hope to follow their dreams.
It helps people think of special moments in the past.
optionsZh: 讓人們獲得書作為禮物
幫人們節省買新書的錢
幫助人們回憶過去的特別時刻
questions.explanation: 書的主人拿回修復後的舊書，「感覺被帶回了和老師相處的時光」，說明陳的工作喚起珍貴記憶，故選 (D)。

questions.question: What is his magic?
options: Finding books that are long lost.
Changing his bookstore into a library.
questions.explanation: 文章末句：「看到書主人的微笑讓陳快樂，因為那樣的微笑正是他的魔法所為」，故選 (B)。

passages.passage:
In Austria, there are not many restaurants like Habibi & Hawara. Here you can enjoy Austrian and Syrian food together. Two Austrians, Martin Rohla and David Kreytenberg, started this yummy plan from their experience of working with refugees.

Every year, thousands of people have to leave their homes and families because of war in their countries. So many of them go to Austria that some people in the country think they have become a problem. They are afraid refugees may take away their jobs and they don't feel safe around them, either. But Rohla and Kreytenberg would beg to differ: The refugees don't have to be a problem. They want to help them start a new life. They invited refugees to meetings and found that some of them were cooks back in Syria. So the idea of Habibi & Hawara was born—a restaurant that brings together Austrian and Syrian food. "The two worlds far away from each other meet together," Kreytenberg said.

But Rohla and Kreytenberg want to do more than just give the refugees work. They want their best workers to buy the restaurant in the end. They hope that Habibi & Hawara is not just a one-time plan and that other Austrians will join them and help more refugees.

questions.question: According to the reading, why did Rohla and Kreytenberg open Habibi & Hawara?
options: To help refugees live better in Austria.
To collect money to help Syria fight the war.
To help Austrians learn about the war in Syria.
To help refugees go back to their home countries.
optionsZh: 幫助難民在奧地利過得更好
幫助奧地利人了解敘利亞的戰爭
questions.explanation: 兩位創辦人想幫難民展開新生活（help them start a new life），開設融合兩國料理的餐廳提供就業機會，故選 (A)。

questions.question: What can we learn about Habibi & Hawara?
options: It was moved from Syria to Austria.
It may finally be sold to its workers.
It has cooking classes in Syrian food.
It is an important meeting place for Syrians.
optionsZh: 有敘利亞料理烹飪課程
是敘利亞人重要的聚會場所
questions.explanation: 文中提到他們希望讓最優秀的員工最終買下餐廳（buy the restaurant in the end），故選 (B)。

questions.question: What does it mean when people "beg to differ"?
optionsZh: 他們無法代表他人發言
他們沒有注意到某件事
questions.explanation: beg to differ 是禮貌表達異議的慣用語，意思是「我不同意（你的看法）」，故選 (A)。

passages.passage:
If you want to learn about bringing back extinct animals, reading John Smith's Back from the Dead is a good start. But I have to say I can't agree with everything he says.

From his book, I can see Smith is an advocate of bringing extinct animals back. He says we have lost many animals because we made their living spaces too dirty. He also thinks we must do everything we can to bring them back. However, I don't know how much time and money we would need to make the world a better place for them, and honestly no one knows if they could actually come back.

Smith also says that bringing back extinct animals may be good for us. He talks about an extinct frog that grew tadpoles in its stomach. Some women's babies die before they are born, and Smith believes bringing back the frog may be an answer to the problem. According to him, by studying how the tadpoles grow inside the frog's stomach, we might be able to find new ways to help these women successfully keep their babies. But I don't think things are as easy as Smith thinks. We have spent ten years and millions of dollars on this kind of frog, but all we have got are only a few dead frog eggs. If we can't see a real frog, how can we be sure women will get the help they need?

Dr. Solomon Wang from the Animal Saving Office says the study of bringing back extinct animals is "throwing good money after bad." And he's right. A lot of hard work has been put into this expensive dream, but we haven't seen anything come out of it. So should we still keep going down this road?

—Ellen Zimmer

questions.question: What does it mean when someone is an advocate of something?
options: They talk a lot but do little about it.
They believe it is good and should be done.
They have had some bad experiences with it.
They are one of the first people who have done it.
optionsZh: 相信某事是好的並應該去做
是最早去做這件事的人之一
questions.explanation: 文中 Smith 是倡議者（advocate），他認為復育滅絕動物是好事且應積極推動，故選 (B)。

questions.question: What is special about the extinct frog in Smith's book?
options: It can live in a dirty living space.
It might help fix a health problem.
It eats its babies when it cannot find food.
It is the first extinct animal that people studied.
optionsZh: 可能有助於解決健康問題
找不到食物時會吃掉幼崽
是人類研究的第一種滅絕動物
questions.explanation: 書中提到研究這種在胃中孵化蝌蚪的滅絕青蛙，可能有助於解決女性流產問題，故選 (B)。

questions.question: Why does Ellen Zimmer use the words from Dr. Solomon Wang?
options: To invite people to take action.
optionsZh: 讓她的觀點更清晰有力
questions.explanation: Zimmer 引用 Dr. Wang「花錢如打水漂」的說法，強化自己對復育計畫持懷疑立場的論點，故選 (C)。

questions.question: What does Ellen Zimmer most likely think about bringing back extinct animals?
questions.explanation: Zimmer 花了大量金錢卻只得到幾顆死蛙卵，質疑計畫能否成功，整體持強烈懷疑態度，認為難以實現，故選 (C)。

passages.caption: 1918年流感疫情期間美國四城市每10萬人死亡人數比較圖（含社交距離實施期間）

passages.passage:
Since the Covid-19 pandemic started, people have begun "social distancing"—keeping a safe space between you and anyone you don't live with. Though it has been a very popular topic this year, social distancing __(40)__. In fact, it was widely used in the U.S. in the flu pandemic in 1918. But how well did it work? The Americans' experience can tell us whether it really saved lives.

From the picture, we can see that __(41)__ started social distancing earlier than the other three cities. This helped the city keep the number of deaths down. Besides taking early action, keeping the action long enough was also important in fighting the pandemic. The picture shows that Portland and Denver did well in the first few weeks. However, both cities __(42)__, and the numbers of deaths climbed again.

But there was one thing Portland did right. The city used several ways of social distancing at the same time to deal with the pandemic. __(43)__, sick people were kept away from healthy ones; schools were closed; public activities were not allowed. All these were useful in keeping people safe.

The lessons of the 1918 pandemic bring us hope. While we are still learning to fight Covid-19, social distancing may be the best way to get us through the dark days, at least for now.

questions.question: Though it has been a very popular topic this year, social distancing ___.
questions.explanation: 後文提到社交距離早在1918年流感疫情中就廣泛使用，說明它並非新概念，故選 (A)。

questions.question: From the picture, we can see that ___ started social distancing earlier than the other three cities.
questions.explanation: 圖表顯示 New York 最早開始實施社交距離，且維持最久，24週後死亡人數最低（453人），故選 (B)。

questions.question: However, both cities ___, and the numbers of deaths climbed again.
options: began social distancing a second time
had the most days of social distancing
optionsZh: 太早停止社交距離措施
第二次開始實施社交距離
實施社交距離天數最多
questions.explanation: Portland 和 Denver 在最初幾週表現良好，但後來死亡人數又上升，表示他們太早停止了社交距離措施，故選 (B)。

questions.question: ___, sick people were kept away from healthy ones; schools were closed; public activities were not allowed.
questions.explanation: 這句話列舉 Portland 同時採用多種社交距離方式的具體例子，用 For example 引導，故選 (D)。
